values.content:
Normalize invoice data from OCR markdown into strict JSON.

Input boundary:
- OCR markdown is already extracted by Mistral OCR.
- Do NOT perform OCR.
- Do NOT reconstruct tables differently from OCR.
- Preserve OCR row boundaries exactly.
- Never merge multiple printed rows into one row.
- Never hallucinate missing line items.

Mistral OCR responsibility (already done):
- text extraction
- layout detection
- table structure preservation
- row/column detection
- reading order

Your responsibility:
- classify document_type
- normalize fields
- structure JSON
- preserve row boundaries
- extract GST values

Document type rule:
- Compare GSTIN values case-insensitively after trim.
- IF buyer_gstin equals OUR_GSTIN => document_type = "purchase"
- IF vendor_gstin equals OUR_GSTIN => document_type = "sales"
- If both match or neither match or OUR_GSTIN missing => document_type = null

Line-level GST rules:
- If OCR has per-row CGST/SGST/IGST, extract those values per row.
- Never skip line-level GST when row values exist.
- Never distribute summary GST across rows.
- If GST exists only in summary/HSN summary and not per row, set line-item cgst/sgst/igst as null.

Return ONLY raw JSON.
Do not wrap in markdown.
Do not use code fences.
Do not add explanation text.
Never return input-wrapper keys like OUR_GSTIN or OCR_MARKDOWN.
Use null for unknown values.

Required output schema:
{
  "document_type": "purchase"|"sales"|null,
  "vendor_name": string|null,
  "vendor_gstin": string|null,
  "buyer_name": string|null,
  "buyer_gstin": string|null,
  "invoice_number": string|null,
  "invoice_date": string|null,
  "state": string|null,
  "subtotal": number|null,
  "cgst": number|null,
  "sgst": number|null,
  "igst": number|null,
  "tds": number|null,
  "round_off": number|null,
  "total_amount": number|null,
  "line_items": [
    {
      "line_no": number|null,
      "description": string|null,
      "hsn": string|null,
      "quantity": number|null,
      "quantity_raw": string|null,
      "uom": string|null,
      "rate": number|null,
      "taxable_amount": number|null,
      "cgst": number|null,
      "cgst_raw": string|null,
      "sgst": number|null,
      "sgst_raw": string|null,
      "igst": number|null,
      "igst_raw": string|null,
      "total_amount": number|null
    }
  ],
  "hsn_tax_summary": [
    {
      "hsn": string|null,
      "taxable_amount": number|null,
      "cgst_rate": number|null,
      "cgst": number|null,
      "sgst_rate": number|null,
      "sgst": number|null,
      "igst_rate": number|null,
      "igst": number|null
    }
  ]
}

values.content:
Previous normalization response was invalid or truncated.

Re-run normalization from the SAME OCR markdown input.
Do NOT perform OCR.
Do NOT change OCR reading order.
Preserve OCR row boundaries exactly.
Never merge rows.
Never hallucinate missing line items.

Apply the same rules strictly:
- document_type rule using OUR_GSTIN comparison
- line-level GST extraction rules

Output contract (MANDATORY):
- Return EXACTLY one JSON object.
- Output must be VALID minified JSON parseable by JSON.parse.
- No markdown, no code fences, no comments, no prose.
- No trailing commas.
- Close all braces and brackets.
- Use null for unknown values.
- Never return input-wrapper keys like OUR_GSTIN or OCR_MARKDOWN.

Required schema:
{"document_type":"purchase"|"sales"|null,"vendor_name":string|null,"vendor_gstin":string|null,"buyer_name":string|null,"buyer_gstin":string|null,"invoice_number":string|null,"invoice_date":string|null,"state":string|null,"subtotal":number|null,"cgst":number|null,"sgst":number|null,"igst":number|null,"tds":number|null,"round_off":number|null,"total_amount":number|null,"line_items":[{"line_no":number|null,"description":string|null,"hsn":string|null,"quantity":number|null,"quantity_raw":string|null,"uom":string|null,"rate":number|null,"taxable_amount":number|null,"cgst":number|null,"cgst_raw":string|null,"sgst":number|null,"sgst_raw":string|null,"igst":number|null,"igst_raw":string|null,"total_amount":number|null}],"hsn_tax_summary":[{"hsn":string|null,"taxable_amount":number|null,"cgst_rate":number|null,"cgst":number|null,"sgst_rate":number|null,"sgst":number|null,"igst_rate":number|null,"igst":number|null}]}

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ extraction_status: 'FAILED', retry_count: (($json.retryCount ?? 0) + 1), raw_model_output: ($json._meta || {}), extracted_json: ($json || {}), normalized_fields: { invoice_number: ($json.invoice_number || null), invoice_date: ($json.invoice_date || null), party_name: ($json.vendor_name || $json.buyer_name || null), party_gstin: ($json.vendor_gstin || $json.buyer_gstin || null), currency: ($json.currency || 'INR'), subtotal: ($json.subtotal ?? $json.taxable_amount ?? null), taxable_amount: ($json.taxable_amount ?? null), cgst_amount: ($json.cgst ?? null), sgst_amount: ($json.sgst ?? null), igst_amount: ($json.igst ?? null), round_off_amount: ($json.round_off ?? null), total_amount: ($json.total_amount ?? null) }, confidence_score: ($json.confidence_score ?? null), low_confidence_fields: ($json.low_confidence_fields || []), warnings: ($json.review_warnings || []), salvaged: true, error_message: ($json._meta?.error || 'Extraction failed after retry') }) }}

conditions.leftValue: ={{ (() => { const mode = String($json.postingMode || $json.posting_mode || '').toUpperCase(); const forceRaw = $json.forceAutoPost ?? $json.force_auto_post ?? false; const forceText = String(forceRaw).toLowerCase(); const forceAuto = forceRaw === true || forceText === 'true' || forceText === '1'; return mode === 'REVIEW_BEFORE_POSTING' && !forceAuto; })() }}
